---
title: "TDM Run Results"
subtitle: "Wasatch Front Regional Travel Demand Model — Run Set Reporting"
---

```{python}
#| echo: false
import sys
sys.path.insert(0, ".")
import report_data as rd
import pandas as pd
from IPython.display import Markdown, display

# Newest run set first, by each run set's own most recent run -- not
# alphabetical, so a newly-added or newly-updated run set surfaces at top
# rather than wherever its id happens to sort.
run_set_ids = sorted(
    rd.discover_run_set_ids(), key=rd.run_set_latest_run_at, reverse=True
)
nm2023_scenario_count = rd.scenario_count("non-motorized-2023")
```

Generated from committed run metadata under `runs/`, newest run set first.
A new run set appears here automatically once it has at least one committed run.

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
if not run_set_ids:
    print("No run sets have any committed runs yet.")

for rs_id in run_set_ids:
    byline = rd.run_set_byline(rs_id)
    byline_md = f"*{byline}*\n\n" if byline else ""

    if rs_id == "non-motorized-2023":
        display(Markdown(
            f"## non-motorized-2023\n\n{byline_md}"
            f"Non-motorized network sensitivity test — {nm2023_scenario_count} scenarios "
            "varying HH and EMP at different multipliers and spatial scopes to quantify "
            "mode-choice response to localized land-use changes. Manually curated from "
            "pre-existing scenario outputs (not run through the `tdmruns` CLI), so it's "
            "linked directly here rather than through the generic run-metadata table below.\n\n"
            "- [Slides view](run_sets/non-motorized-2023/slides.html)\n"
            "- [Summary view](run_sets/non-motorized-2023/summary.html)\n"
        ))
        continue

    if rs_id == "bring-work-trips-closer-to-home":
        description = rd.run_set_description(rs_id)
        display(Markdown(
            f"## bring-work-trips-closer-to-home\n\n{byline_md}{description}\n\n"
            "- [Slides view](run_sets/bring-work-trips-closer-to-home/slides.html)\n"
            "- [Summary view](run_sets/bring-work-trips-closer-to-home/summary.html)\n"
        ))
        continue

    if rd.has_custom_report_pages(rs_id):
        # A run set with its own hand-written pages that isn't one of the
        # special cases above -- link to its custom directory rather than
        # guessing at a generic run_sets/<id>.qmd that won't exist. Add a
        # dedicated blurb like non-motorized-2023's above once its actual
        # page filenames are known.
        description = rd.run_set_description(rs_id)
        display(Markdown(
            f"## {rs_id}\n\n{byline_md}{description}\n\n"
            f"See `reports/run_sets/{rs_id}/` for this run set's custom report pages.\n"
        ))
        continue

    description = rd.run_set_description(rs_id)
    display(Markdown(f"## [{rs_id}](run_sets/{rs_id}.qmd)\n\n{byline_md}{description}\n"))

    runs = rd.latest_run_per_scenario(rs_id)
    if not runs:
        display(Markdown("_No runs recorded yet._\n"))
        continue

    rows = []
    for run in runs:
        rows.append({
            "Scenario": run["scenario_id"],
            "Status": run["status"],
            "Run": run["run_id"],
            "TDM version": run["tdm"].get("resolved_tag") or run["tdm"]["resolved_commit"][:8],
        })
    display(pd.DataFrame(rows))
```
